motions.title: WestCreek Development Plan Amended for Future Growth
motions.summary: The city council approved changes to the WestCreek area's overall development plan. This update means how land is developed and used in the WestCreek neighborhood will be updated, affecting where future homes, businesses, and public spaces can be built.
motions.full_text: THAT Council give Third Reading to Bylaw No. 25-07, being a Bylaw to amend WestCreek Area Structure Plan No. 08-22.

motions.title: City to Share Costs with School Boards for 2025 Election
motions.summary: The city council decided to work with local school boards to manage and share the costs of the 2025 municipal election. This cooperation will make the election process more coordinated and efficient for residents voting for both city and school board representatives.
motions.full_text: THAT Council authorizes the Returning Officer to enter into an agreement with the Golden Hills School Division #75 and Christ the Redeemer Catholic Schools for the 2025 municipal election, with costs to be shared as referenced in Attachment I.

motions.title: City Initiates Process to Expand Boundaries into Wheatland County
motions.summary: The city council approved the first step towards formally expanding the city's borders by taking over land currently part of Wheatland County. This action means the city intends to grow, which could impact future development, service delivery, and property taxes for residents in or near these areas.
motions.full_text: THAT Council direct staff to provide written notice of its' proposed annexation pursuant to Section 116 of the Municipal Government Act.

motions.title: New Chief Election Officer Appointed for 2025 Municipal Election
motions.summary: The city council changed the person responsible for organizing the 2025 municipal election, appointing Vanessa Cunningham as the new Chief Election Officer. This decision ensures that election-related procedures and staffing will be overseen by new leadership to ensure a smooth democratic process for all residents.
motions.full_text: THAT Council rescind Resolution No. 355.12.24: "THAT Council appoint Johnathan Strathdee as the Town of Strathmore's Returning Officer for the 2025 Municipal Election; AND THAT Council appoint Claudette Thorhaug as the Town of Strathmore's Substitute Returning Officer for the 2025 Municipal Election." THAT Council appoint Vanessa Cunningham as the Town of Strathmore's Returning Officer for the 2025 Municipal Election. THAT Council appoint Claudette Thorhaug as the Town of Strathmore's Substitute Returning Officer for the 2025 Municipal Election.

motions.title: City Approves 2025 Property Tax Rates with School Tax Adjustments
motions.summary: The city council has officially approved the property tax rates for 2025, including specific adjustments to the portion of taxes that funds local school boards. This means your property tax bill for the upcoming year will reflect these new rates, directly affecting how much homeowners and businesses pay.
motions.full_text: THAT Council give Second Reading to Bylaw No. 25-11, being the 2025 Property Tax Rate Bylaw as amended: ·Amend Alberta School Foundation Fund Tax Rates in Section 2 of Bylaw No. 25-11 as follows: Residential/Farmland Tax Rate to 2.47200 (2.47300 Prior) Non-Residential Tax Rate to 3.66900 (3.33300 Prior) Residential (O/C-135/2010) Tax Rate to 2.55330 (2.43200 Prior) Non-Residential (O/C-135/2010) Tax Rate to 3.6865 (3.67900 Prior) Farmland (O/C-135/2010) Tax Rate to 2.5533 (2.43200 Prior) Amend Christ Redeemer Catholic School Division Tax Rates in Section 2 of Bylaw No. 25-11 as follows: Residential/Farmland Tax Rate 2.47200 (2.43200 Prior) 。 oNon-Residential Tax Rate to 3.66900 (3.33300 Prior)

motions.title: City Considers Easing Rules for Garden and Garage Suites
motions.summary: The city council took the initial step to change land use rules that might make it easier to build garden and garage suites (secondary living spaces) on residential properties. A public hearing is now scheduled for June 4th, inviting residents to share their opinions before a final decision is made.
motions.full_text: THAT Council give First reading to Bylaw No. 25-09, being a bylaw to amend Land Use Bylaw No. 14-11; AND THAT Council direct Administration to schedule a Public Hearing for Bylaw No. 25-09 on June 4, 2025 at 7:00 p.m. in Council Chambers.

motions.title: City Proclaims Appreciation Week for Disability Service Workers
motions.summary: The city council officially declared May 19-25, 2025, as Community Disability Services Professional Appreciation Week. This is the city's way of recognizing and celebrating the important work of professionals who provide vital support to individuals with disabilities in our community.
motions.full_text: THAT Council proclaim the week of May 19 - 25, 2025 Community Disability Services Professional Appreciation Week in Strathmore, Alberta.
